children.heading: Credit period
children.label: B Credit period
children.text: In the case of a qualified facility described in subparagraph (A), the 10-year period referred to in subsection (a) shall be treated as beginning on the date the efficiency improvements or additions to capacity are placed in service.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: C Special rule
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(i), an efficiency improvement or addition to capacity shall be treated as placed in service before , if the construction of such improvement or addition begins before such date.January 1, 2025

children.heading: Indian coal production facility
children.label: 10 Indian coal production facility
children.text: The term “Indian coal production facility” means a facility that produces Indian coal.

children.heading: Computation of inflation adjustment factor and reference price
children.label: 2 Computation of inflation adjustment factor and reference price

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, not later than April 1 of each calendar year, determine and publish in the Federal Register the inflation adjustment factor and the reference price for such calendar year in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment factor
children.label: B Inflation adjustment factor
children.text: The term “inflation adjustment factor” means, with respect to a calendar year, a fraction the numerator of which is the GDP implicit price deflator for the preceding calendar year and the denominator of which is the GDP implicit price deflator for the calendar year 1992. The term “GDP implicit price deflator” means the most recent revision of the implicit price deflator for the gross domestic product as computed and published by the Department of Commerce before March 15 of the calendar year.

children.heading: Reference price
children.label: C Reference price
children.text: The term “reference price” means, with respect to a calendar year, the Secretary’s determination of the annual average contract price per kilowatt hour of electricity generated from the same qualified energy resource and sold in the previous year in the United States. For purposes of the preceding sentence, only contracts entered into after , shall be taken into account.December 31, 1989

children.heading: Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.label: 3 Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.text: In the case of a facility in which more than 1 person has an ownership interest, except to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, production from the facility shall be allocated among such persons in proportion to their respective ownership interests in the gross sales from such facility.

children.heading: Related persons
children.label: 4 Related persons
children.text: Persons shall be treated as related to each other if such persons would be treated as a single employer under the regulations prescribed under section 52(b). In the case of a corporation which is a member of an affiliated group of corporations filing a consolidated return, such corporation shall be treated as selling electricity to an unrelated person if such electricity is sold to such a person by another member of such group.

children.heading: Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.label: 5 Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) of section 52 shall apply.

children.heading: Application of rules
children.label: C Application of rules
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of the subsection (b)(3) and paragraphs (1) through (5) of this subsection shall apply for purposes of determining the amount of any increase under this paragraph.

children.label: I Credit amount
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting “$2 per barrel-of-oil equivalent” for “$4.375 per ton”.

children.heading: Credit period
children.label: II Credit period
children.text: In lieu of the 10-year period referred to in clauses (i) and (ii)(II) of subparagraph (A), the credit period shall be the period beginning on the later of the date such facility was originally placed in service, the date the modifications described in clause (iii) were placed in service, or , and ending on the later of , or the date which is 1 year after the date such facility or the modifications described in clause (iii) were placed in service.October 1, 2008December 31, 2009

children.heading: No phaseout
children.label: III No phaseout
children.text: Subparagraph (B) shall not apply.

children.heading: Modifications
children.label: iii Modifications
children.text: The modifications described in this clause are modifications to an existing facility which allow such facility to produce steel industry fuel.

children.heading: Barrel-of-oil equivalent
children.label: iv Barrel-of-oil equivalent
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, a barrel-of-oil equivalent is the amount of steel industry fuel that has a Btu content of 5,800,000 Btus.

children.heading: Coordination with credit for producing fuel from a nonconventional source
children.label: 9 Coordination with credit for producing fuel from a nonconventional source

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified facility” shall not include any facility which produces electricity from gas derived from the biodegradation of municipal solid waste if such biodegradation occurred in a facility (within the meaning of section 45K) the production from which is allowed as a credit under section 45K for the taxable year or any prior taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “refined coal production facility” shall not include any facility the production from which is allowed as a credit under section 45K for the taxable year or any prior taxable year (or under section 29, as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of the Energy Tax Incentives Act of 2005, for any prior taxable year).3See References in Text note below.3

children.heading: Exception for steel industry coal
children.label: ii Exception for steel industry coal
children.text: In the case of a facility producing steel industry fuel, clause (i) shall not apply to so much of the refined coal produced at such facility as is steel industry fuel.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment
children.label: ii Inflation adjustment
children.text: In the case of any calendar year after 2006, each of the dollar amounts under clause (i) shall be equal to the product of such dollar amount and the inflation adjustment factor determined under paragraph (2)(B) for the calendar year, except that such paragraph shall be applied by substituting “2005” for “1992”.

children.heading: Application of rules
children.label: C Application of rules
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of the subsection (b)(3) and paragraphs (1), (3), (4), and (5) of this subsection shall apply for purposes of determining the amount of any increase under this paragraph.

children.heading: Allocation of credit to patrons of agricultural cooperative
children.label: 11 Allocation of credit to patrons of agricultural cooperative

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of an eligible cooperative organization, any portion of the credit determined under subsection (a) for the taxable year may, at the election of the organization, be apportioned among patrons of the organization on the basis of the amount of business done by the patrons during the taxable year.

children.heading: Form and effect of election
children.label: ii Form and effect of election
children.text: An election under clause (i) for any taxable year shall be made on a timely filed return for such year. Such election, once made, shall be irrevocable for such taxable year. Such election shall not take effect unless the organization designates the apportionment as such in a written notice mailed to its patrons during the payment period described in section 1382(d).

children.label: B Treatment of organizations and patrons

children.heading: Eligible cooperative defined
children.label: D Eligible cooperative defined
children.text: For purposes of this section the term “eligible cooperative” means a cooperative organization described in section 1381(a) which is owned more than 50 percent by agricultural producers or by entities owned by agricultural producers. For this purpose an entity owned by an agricultural producer is one that is more than 50 percent owned by agricultural producers.

children.heading: Coordination with energy credit for qualified biogas property
children.label: 12 Coordination with energy credit for qualified biogas property
children.text: The term “qualified facility” shall not include any facility which produces electricity from gas produced by qualified biogas property (as defined in section 48(c)(7)) if a credit is allowed under section 48 with respect to such property for the taxable year or any prior taxable year.
